target: PHIL-10: Swarm learning compounds through persistent artifacts
expect: Citation rate per 50-session window shows non-monotonic pattern (at least one recovery). PHIL-10 survives if compounding evidence found.
actual: Three tests: (1) Citation rate per 50-session window: NON-MONOTONIC, 10 recoveries across 16 windows. Rate 1.29→5.08 overall. DROP criterion NOT met. (2) Citation density per 100-session era: INCREASING (2.29→3.67→4.62). (3) Backward reach: NOT increasing — median gap 56→29 sessions. Old-knowledge fraction 57.5%→30.6%.
diff: PHIL-10 PARTIALLY CONFIRMED. Compounding real (density increasing, rate non-monotonic) but backward reach declining — newer lessons cite recent lessons, not old ones. Compounding within ~50-session horizon, not corpus-wide.
verdict: PARTIALLY CONFIRMED — compounding real but horizon-bounded
refine: Add horizon qualifier: compounding within ~50-session attention window, not corpus-wide.
